questionText: ROUND TWO (Problem of the Day) You went to a Fuel Service Station to buy a litre of kerosene but you were informed that the stock available was contaminated with some benzoic acid. You need the kerosene to perform an experiment so you are forced to buy the impure one with the intention of purifying it. You cannot do a distillation but you can purify it by another method of your choice. How would you purify the kerosene? Provide a list of all materials including chemicals needed for the purification. (C) Solution Apparatus/Chemicals Separating funnel, conical or cylindrical Glass funnel Beaker Conical flask Glass rod Filter paper NaOH or KOH solution Anhydrous Na2SO4 or MgSO4 Procedure Transfer the impure kerosene into a separating funnel using a glass/simple funnel. Add about 100 cm3 of NaOH or KOH solution to the kerosene. Stopper the funnel and shake vigorously. Drain out the aqueous layer and add fresh NaOH or KOH solution. Shake and drain the aqueous layer. Wash the kerosene about three times, each time with about 50 cm3 of water. Drain the kerosene into a conical flask. Add anhydrous Na2SO4 or MgSO4 until the solid can move freely when the flask is swirled around. Filter the kerosene into a suitable, clean container through a fluted filter paper and stopper the container. ROUND THREE (True or False) Q. Adjacent angles on a straight line are supplementary. (M)

questionText: ROUND TWO (Problem of the Day) (P) Sketch a ray diagram showing light incident on, passing through, and emerging from a 60° glass prism. Show the case of minimum deviation and normal incidence. Explain why for parallel beam of white light, minimum deviation occurs only for one wavelength. Solution At minimum deviation, light passes symmetrically through the prism. For normal incidence on a 60° prism made of ordinary glass (n~1.5), light is totally reflected at one face because. The refractive index of glass depends on wavelength and the condition for minimum deviation can be fulfilled for only one wavelength if a polychromatic beam is incident on the prism. Explain how high altitude will affect the performance of the athletes. Solution As one moves up a higher altitude, the air becomes thinner. Thinner air means less resistance, so the sprinters are expected to perform better at high altitude. However the thinner air also means reduction in the amount of oxygen, hence the pace of the endurance athletes which depends on high rates of oxygen consumption gets slower at high altitude. questionText: ROUND TWO (Problem of the Day) (C) You have been provided all the apparatus needed to prepare a column for column chromatograph to separate two coloured compounds. Describe how the column will be set up. Solution Procedure The glass column is washed thoroughly with detergent, rinsed and dried in the oven at 110-120°C. The column is clamped upright, on a retort stand. A small plug of cotton or glass wool is pushed down to the bottom of the column. The column is filled with petroleum ether (boiling range 60-80°C). A suitable quantity of the adsorbent is weighed in a beaker. A small volume of petroleum ether is added and the mixture stirred to give a slurry. Using a funnel, and while stirring, the slurry is added, small quantities at a time to the column whose tap is left open. The column is gently tapped with the glass rod to prevent any air bubble being formed or trapped. When all the slurry has been added, there should be some solvent on top of the adsorbent. The tap is now closed until the column is about to be used. ROUND THREE (True or False) Q. The electric field of two equal charges has maximum magnitude at the centre of the line joining the charges.
